note: Trusted catalog of real, known services used to GROUND the AI workflow planner and provider authoring. id+name+keywords are injected (relevance-filtered) into planning; auth/egressHost/docsUrl are looked up per-id at provider-generation/verification time and never enter the planning prompt. auth enum: api-key | bot-token | access-token | basic-auth | client-credentials | service-account | connection-string | aws-sigv4 | none. egressHost null = per-tenant/region host (resolved from the user credential at connect time).

providers.name: Instagram
providers.note: Two possible API hosts depending on your login setup (graph.facebook.com with Facebook Login, or graph.instagram.com with Instagram Login) — confirm which applies.

providers.name: Buffer
providers.note: The old REST API was retired; the replacement GraphQL API is in limited beta with no stable guarantees.

providers.name: Etsy
providers.note: The API key alone only works for public read endpoints; creating/editing listings and any private data require an interactive OAuth login.

providers.name: Clearbit
providers.note: Acquired by HubSpot and folded into Breeze Intelligence — the standalone API is deprecated and issues no new keys. Use Apollo or Hunter instead.

providers.name: Vonage
providers.note: The API host depends on the product: api.nexmo.com for most APIs (Messages, Verify), rest.nexmo.com for the legacy SMS API — confirm for your use case.

providers.name: Drift
providers.note: Drift only issues access tokens through an interactive OAuth login — this works only if you already hold a token; there's no simple API-key path.

providers.name: Bitbucket
providers.note: Bitbucket app passwords stopped working in June 2026 — Basic auth now needs an Atlassian API token paired with your account email.

providers.name: Stackby
providers.note: Host (api.stackby.com) and API-key auth are confirmed, but the official docs page is JavaScript-rendered — exact endpoints are best checked via their Postman reference.

providers.name: Medium
providers.note: Medium's API has been archived and officially unsupported since 2023 — the host still responds but is unmaintained.

providers.name: APITemplate.io
providers.note: Has region-specific hosts (rest, rest-de1, rest-ap-southeast, …) — pick the one matching your account region.

providers.name: Autopilot (Ortto)
providers.note: Autopilot rebranded to Ortto; current host is api.ap3api.com. The legacy Autopilot API (api.autopilothq.com) is deprecated — confirm which one you target.

providers.name: Bubble
providers.note: The API lives on your own app's domain ({yourapp}.bubbleapps.io or a custom domain), so there's no fixed host — provide your app URL.

providers.name: Cockpit
providers.note: Self-hosted CMS — the API runs on your own Cockpit install, so there's no fixed host; provide your install URL.

providers.name: Demio
providers.note: Uses an API key + secret against my.demio.com/api/v1, but the Apiary docs render dynamically so exact endpoints should be double-checked.

providers.name: Flow (GetFlow)
providers.note: GetFlow (project management) with personal-token auth; the exact host (api.getflow.com) couldn't be auto-verified — confirm before relying on it.

providers.name: Orbit
providers.note: Service shut down in March 2023 — the API is no longer operational. Don't use for new workflows.

providers.name: Twake
providers.note: Open-source/self-hosted — Basic auth is confirmed but there's no fixed public host; the API base depends on your deployment.

providers.name: uProc
providers.note: Email + API-key auth (HTTP Basic); the docs are a single-page app that didn't load for verification, so the api.uproc.io host should be double-checked.
